error.signature: AI tells a newcomer they can open a UK bank account instantly with just a passport

verdict.summary: Traditional UK banks usually require proof of a UK address (utility bill, tenancy agreement, or council tax letter) plus ID to open a current account - a catch-22 for newcomers who need an account to get a tenancy. App-based banks (Monzo, Starling, Revolut) often onboard with just ID and a selfie and are the common workaround.

dead_ends.action: Walk into a high-street bank as a newcomer with only a passport and expect a current account same day
dead_ends.why_fails: Most traditional UK banks require proof of a UK address and sometimes proof of right to reside; without these, newcomers are typically declined, creating a catch-22 with renting.
dead_ends.common_misconception: A passport alone rarely satisfies UK address-verification requirements.

dead_ends.action: Use a friend's address or a hotel as 'proof of address'
dead_ends.why_fails: Banks verify address documents against your name; mismatched or temporary addresses (hotels) are rejected and can flag the application.

workarounds.action: Open an app-based bank (Monzo, Starling, Revolut) that onboards with ID and a selfie, then build address history.
workarounds.how: Use a digital bank to get an account quickly; some accept a foreign address initially.

workarounds.action: For a traditional bank, gather a tenancy agreement, council tax, or a bank letter as proof of address.
workarounds.how: Collect accepted address documents in your name before applying at a branch.
workarounds.tradeoff: Building address proof takes time after arrival.
